name: Chicken Andouille Gumbo
tagline: Dark-roux gumbo with chicken, andouille, and the holy trinity
highlight: The roux is the soul — chocolate-dark, no soy, no shortcuts

hero.alt: Bowl of dark gumbo with chicken, sausage, and rice
hero.caption: gumbo — chocolate roux

healthFacts: A dark roux loses thickening power but multiplies flavor — Cajun magic.
Filé powder (sassafras) thickens off-heat and adds an earthy note.

steps.title: Build the dark roux
steps.detail: Whisk flour and oil in a heavy pot over medium-low. Stir constantly 25–35 min until the color of milk chocolate. Don't walk away.
steps.tip: Burnt roux is bitter and there's no fixing it. Patience.

steps.title: Add the trinity
steps.detail: Toss in onion, celery, bell pepper. Stir 5 min — they'll stop the roux from getting darker. Add garlic 1 min.
steps.tip: Cold vegetables actually halt roux color, which is the trick.

steps.title: Build the gumbo
steps.detail: Slowly whisk in stock. Add bay leaves, andouille, chicken, salt. Bring to a simmer.
steps.tip: Add stock in stages and whisk hard — lumps form fast.

steps.title: Slow and finish
steps.detail: Simmer 50 min, skimming any surface fat. Off heat, sprinkle filé powder; stir. Serve over rice.
steps.tip: Filé only off heat — boiling makes it stringy.
